data.title: Stock Market News Today, 1/30/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Fall after Microsoft Drags Down Nasdaq and S&P
data.description:
U.S. stock futures fell early Friday after a 10% share price drop in the tech giant Microsoft (MSFT) dragged down the Nasdaq and S&P 500. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 1.01%, 0.80%, and 0.76%, respectively, at 3:24 a.m. EST on January 30.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Thursday's regular trading, the three major indexes closed mixed. The S&P 500 closed down 0.13%, the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite dropped 0.72%, and the Dow Jones Industrial Average gained 0.11%.

After the market closed, Apple (AAPL) shares jumped after exceeding fiscal first-quarter expectations, backed by robust iPhone sales. The company guided for revenue to grow between 13% and 16% year-over-year in Q2FY26. Moreover, SanDisk (SNDK) stock rallied 17% after beating Q2FY26 estimates and giving solid guidance.

Looking forward, investors will watch closely as President Donald Trump announces his pick to succeed Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell on Friday morning, heightening market uncertainty.

On the earnings front, results from SoFi Technologies (SOFI), ExxonMobil (XOM), Verizon (VZ), Chevron (CVX), and American Express (AXP) will be released today.

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.27%. WTI crude oil futures were trending lower, hovering near $64.14 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price decreased to nearly $5,154 per ounce on Friday, stalling a long-running rally.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened mixed today amid a flood of corporate earnings releases.

Asia-Pacific Markets Trade Lower

Asia-Pacific markets traded mostly lower on January 30, tracking Wall Street losses.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index fell 2.07%. In China, the Shanghai Composite declined 0.96%, while the Shenzhen Component lost 1.32%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei slid 0.10%, but the Topix rose 0.59%.

Stay ahead of macro events with our up-to-the-minute Economic Calendar — filter by impact, country, and more.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: 'We Can't Ignore It,' Says Top Analyst about Broadcom; Upgrades AVGO Stock to Buy
data.description:
Broadcom Limited (AVGO) stock got a bullish upgrade from Wolfe Research after the firm raised its rating to Buy from Neutral and set a $400 price target, implying a 21% upside from current levels. The firm's top analyst Chris Caso said the call reflects fast-growing demand for Alphabet's (GOOGL) Tensor Processing Units (TPUs).

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"We can no longer ignore the growth and strength of TPUs," Caso said, adding that checks suggest annual shipments could reach 7 million units by 2028.

Why Caso Upgraded AVGO Stock to Buy

Caso said Google's decision to offer TPUs to outside customers marks a major shift in the AI chip market. As a result, TPUs are emerging as a real alternative to Nvidia's (NVDA) GPUs, positioning Broadcom as one of the key beneficiaries of this change.

Based on this view, Wolfe raised its long-term outlook for the company. The firm now expects Broadcom to generate $154.5 billion in revenue and $16 in earnings per share by 2027, reflecting stronger demand for AI-related chips. Caso also noted that there could be further upside from additional AI programs that are not yet fully reflected in current estimates.

In line with this view, Wolfe increased its 2026 AI chip revenue forecast to about $44 billion, driven by roughly 3.3 million TPU shipments. Looking ahead, the firm now sees 2027 AI revenue rising to $78.4 billion, supported by expected shipments of around 5.1 million units.

Beyond chips, networking is another important growth driver. Wolfe expects AI-related networking revenue to rise 75% in 2026, followed by 55% growth in 2027, as data-center spending remains strong.

Valuation Still Looks Reasonable

Despite the strong rally in AI stocks, Wolfe believes Broadcom's valuation remains reasonable. Caso said the company is well positioned to benefit from continued growth in AI spending over the coming years. 

Is AVGO a Good Stock to Buy? 

Turning to Wall Street, AVGO stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 29 Buys, one Hold, and zero Sell ratings. The average AVGO price target is $457.75, implying 38.41% upside potential in 2026.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Raymond James Turns Bullish on SanDisk Stock (SNDK), Says 'Arguably One of the Most Delayed Upgrades in History'
data.description:
SanDisk (SNDK) stock rallied 20% in Friday's pre-market trading after the data storage device maker reported market-beating Q2 FY26 results and issued a solid outlook, driven by data center-led demand. Following the Q2 print, top Raymond James analyst Melissa Fairbanks upgraded SNDK stock to Buy from Hold with a price target of $725, calling the move "arguably one of the most delayed upgrades in history."

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Raymond James Analyst Says Buy SanDisk Stock

While SanDisk has rallied 127% year-to-date (as of Thursday's closing), Fairbanks expects further upside, driven by strong demand and higher pricing from an "unprecedented" data center and AI cycle. Fairbanks added that SanDisk's demand is "exceptionally strong and likely only growing," as supply tightens to the level where it could be sold out for the year.

Furthermore, the 5-star analyst expects limited new supply to come online in the near term, which should support continued strength in pricing. While Fairbanks has recently favored DRAM suppliers, she now sees a more sustainable, encouraging environment for NAND as well. Consequently, Fairbanks views SanDisk as a major beneficiary of a mix-shift toward longer-lived data center builds, with supply being prioritized away from faster-turn edge and consumer markets.

Morgan Stanley Boosts Price Target

Additionally, Morgan Stanley analyst Joseph Moore boosted the price target for SanDisk stock to $690 from $483 and reiterated a Buy rating, saying, "This is the strongest upcycle we have seen in our 30 years covering the memory industry and SanDisk results tonight are yet another reminder of that."

Moore noted that with SanDisk's Q3 FY26 top-line outlook of more than 52% sequential growth, pricing has to be up 60% or more sequentially. The 5-star analyst contended that the 15% quarter-over-quarter growth in margins, which is much less than the 60% growth in the average selling price (ASP), indicates that the $13 EPS guidance (2.5x consensus) is conservative.

IS SNDK Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Overall, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on SanDisk stock based on 12 Buys and four Holds. The average SNDK stock price target of $455.31 indicates 16% downside risk.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: 3 Stocks at 52-Week Lows Analysts Say Could Jump Over 60%
data.description:
Several well-known companies have slipped to their 52-week lows in the previous trading session, weighed down by market volatility, shifting investor sentiment, and company-specific challenges. However, some of these beaten-down names still have solid fundamentals and long-term growth drivers, suggesting there may be upside potential once market conditions stabilize.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Below are three beaten-down stocks with a 'Strong Buy' consensus and more than 60% upside potential, according to analysts.

  1. Intuit (INTU): Shares of Intuit are slipping as investors reassess growth prospects for software companies that rely on consumer and small‑business spending. Despite the pullback, the company continues to generate steady revenue from its key platforms, TurboTax, QuickBooks, and Credit Karma.

    With ongoing investments in AI automation and financial management tools, analysts believe Intuit's long-term outlook remains solid. The recent drop in the stock may just be short-term sentiment, not a sign that the company's fundamentals are weakening.

    The stock has earned an analyst consensus of Strong Buy. INTU stock's average price target of $806.35 implies a 60.32% upside potential from the current level.
     
  2. Strategy (MSTR): Strategy has had a tough year, dealing with restructuring and changing demand. The stock hitting a 52-week low shows investor caution, but recent improvements suggest the company may finally be turning a corner.

    Cost-cutting measures, focus on core offerings, and early signs of margin recovery have caught analysts' attention. If Strategy continues to deliver on its turnaround plan, the stock could see upside as investor confidence returns.

    MSTR stock has an analyst consensus of Strong Buy. The average price target of $439.36 implies an upside potential of 206.84%. 
  3. ServiceNow (NOW): ServiceNow's decline to a fresh 52-week low is mostly due to weakness across the tech sector. The workflow automation leader continues to post strong subscription growth, expand its enterprise customer base, and roll out new AI capabilities.

    With digital transformation spending expected to rise over the next few years, many analysts view ServiceNow's sell-off as overdone. Its long-term growth story remains compelling, making the current valuation potentially attractive for investors willing to wait.

    The stock has an analyst consensus of Strong Buy. NOW stock's average price target of $195.13 implies an upside potential of 67.17%.

data.title: Goldman Sachs Picks 2 Stocks That Let Investors Buy the Dip or Ride the Momentum
data.description:
In stock trading, wins and losses hinge on countless moving parts, but choosing the right strategy can tip the odds in your favor. That sounds simple enough in theory, yet in practice, it's anything but. To see why, consider two of the market's most popular – and opposing – approaches: buying the dip and riding momentum.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Both do exactly what their names imply. Buying the dip means scooping up beaten-down stocks that still boast solid fundamentals. Riding momentum, on the other hand, is about jumping aboard stocks already on the rise and betting the rally still has fuel left in the tank. Either way, you're swimming against the current – buying when others are selling, or wagering that "past performance doesn't guarantee future results" won't apply this time.

The appeal of these strategies lies in the logic behind them; strong companies can stumble temporarily, creating opportunity for patient investors. Meanwhile, sustained rallies can signal deeper strength and further upside ahead. The real challenge is knowing when each strategy is likely to work – and when it's a trap.

That's never an easy call, but the analysts at Goldman Sachs think they've spotted one stock that fits each playbook in today's market. Using data from the TipRanks database, we took a closer look at both names. Here's what Goldman sees – and why these two stocks stand out.

Spotify Technology (SPOT)

We'll start with a stock on a dip, Spotify. This is the popular music-streaming company from Sweden that has, over the past 20 years, propelled itself into a leading position in the online music streaming business. Spotify is known for its subscription model, its large library of available music and media, and its large audience.

The company offers free users a basic level of access and function, while paying subscribers can dip into a library that includes over 100 million songs, 7 million podcasts, and a growing list of audiobooks. In its report for 3Q25, Spotify announced that it had reached a total of 713 million monthly active users (MAUs), a key metric for an online subscription service. This figure was up 11% year-over-year, and included 281 million premium subscribers. The premium subscriber figure was up 12% year-over-year.

The company was founded in 2006 and went public in 2018. In recent months, the shares have been sliding downward – SPOT has lost approximately 35% since peaking last June. A number of issues have caused investors to pull back, including Spotify's listing of 'AI artists' on its platform and some controversy regarding royalty payments to copyright holders, while slower revenue growth hasn't helped matters either.

We should note that Spotify has been raising its subscription prices, and earlier this month announced that, as of the coming February billing period, the new subscription price will rise from $11.99 per month to $12.99.

For Goldman analyst Eric Sheridan, the new subscription pricing and the increase in regular users are only two of the reasons to see strength in Spotify. He writes ,"Looking over a long-term horizon, we see SPOT as well positioned against several long-term operating themes (which are reflected in our above-Street estimates) in the coming years. Among those themes would be: 1) steady Premium subscription price increases (at a regular cadence of 1-2 years and staggered by geography); 2) the introduction of new premium pricing tiers; 3) healthy MAU growth (particularly in emerging markets) and steady conversion of ad-supported users into paid subscribers; & 4) reacceleration of advertising revenue growth in '26 & beyond, aided by both the launch/scaling of the Spotify Ad Exchange (SAX) and programmatic ad buying capabilities and monetization of video podcast ad inventory."

Summing up, Sheridan adds, "In addition, we see a solid runway for gross profit dollar growth & margin improvement driven by: 1) modestly improving unit economics on core Music product including leverage on royalty payments; 2) leverage on 'fixed' expenses within cost of revenues, most notably Podcast expenses; & 3) scaling of high incremental gross margin revenue streams in Advertising."

Taken all together, these comments back up Sheridan's Buy rating on SPOT, while his $700 price target implies a one-year upside of 39%. (To watch Sheridan's track record, click here)

Spotify has earned a Strong Buy consensus rating on the Street, based on 25 analyst reviews that include 19 Buys and 6 Holds. The stock is trading for $503.92 and its $717.7 average target price is actually more bullish than the Goldman view here, implying a gain of 42% in the next 12 months. (See SPOT stock forecast)
data.author: Michael Marcus

data.title: AstraZeneca (AZN) Moves Deeper into Weight Loss Market with New $18.5B CSPC Agreement
data.description:
AstraZeneca (AZN) is making a major push into the fast-growing obesity drug market. The U.K. pharmaceutical giant has signed a collaboration and licensing deal with China's CSPC Pharmaceuticals, potentially valued at up to $18.5 billion. This deal grants AZN rights to develop and sell CSPC's long-acting peptide medicines and access to its LiquidGel platform for once-monthly injectable dosing outside Greater China and Taiwan.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The move comes as the company looks to diversify its portfolio beyond cancer and respiratory drugs. Also, it sees obesity treatments as one of the major new opportunities in healthcare.

Also, the alliance was announced alongside a broader $15 billion investment plan by AstraZeneca to advance manufacturing and R&D within China.

Here's What the Agreement Includes

According to the terms of the deal, AstraZeneca will make a $1.2 billion upfront payment. Also, CSPC is eligible to receive up to $17.3 billion in additional payments if certain development, regulatory, and sales milestones are met. Further, CSPC will receive tiered, double-digit royalties on net sales outside of China. In return, the partnership gives AZN global rights to Eccogene's experimental oral GLP‑1 therapy.

Importantly, the collaboration includes eight programs and provides access to CSPC's proprietary LiquidGel platform, which enables once-monthly injectable dosing, an approach that could boost patient adherence compared to daily or weekly injections.

With the obesity drug market expected to surpass $100 billion in the next decade, AstraZeneca is positioning itself to compete with leaders such as Novo Nordisk (NVO) and Eli Lilly (LLY).

Is AZN a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Currently, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on AstraZeneca stock based on four Buy recommendations. The average AZN stock price target of $106.75 implies an upside potential of 15.29%.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Why Are Quantum Computing Stocks Plunging? RGTI, IONQ, QBTS Lead Declines
data.description:
Shares of American quantum computing firms, including IonQ Inc. (IONQ), Rigetti Computing (RGTI), D-Wave Quantum (QBTS), and Quantum Computing (QUBT), have been falling after Canada's Xanadu Quantum Technologies inched forward with its U.S. listing plans. Shares are trending down significantly in pre-market trading at the time of writing. Xanadu filed a Form F-4 with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) on January 27, advancing its path to becoming a publicly traded player on the Nasdaq.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Xanadu is a photonic quantum computing firm, which claims its technology offers several advantages over competitors. The filing backs a proposed $3.1 billion business combination with Crane Harbor Acquisition Corp. (CHAC), slated to close in Q1 2026 subject to approvals. The company expects to raise nearly $500 million from the SPAC deal. Upon completion, Xanadu would emerge as the first pure-play photonic quantum computing firm listed on public markets.

How Is Xanadu Different from Others?

Xanadu builds quantum computers using photons, light particles, for computations. Xanadu's X-Series is the first cloud-accessible photonic quantum computer. Xanadu aims to reach one million qubits by linking multiple systems through optical networks.

Its photonic approach delivers standout advantages:

  • Operates at room temperature
  • Easier to manufacture
  • Flexible error correction
  • Modular design
  • Scalable via optical networking
  • Fast processing speeds
  • Compatible with existing telecom infrastructure

Xanadu's technology differs from competitors like IonQ's trapped ions, Rigetti's superconducting qubits that demand ultra-cold temperatures, and D-Wave's quantum annealing with superconducting qubits tuned for optimization tasks rather than universal computing.

Xanadu has also secured up to CAD $23 million via Canada's Quantum Champions Program and advanced to Stage B in DARPA's Quantum Benchmarking Initiative.

Which Is the Better Quantum Computing Stock?

We used the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool to determine which quantum computing stock is most favored by analysts. Over the past five days, IONQ has dropped nearly 9%, RGTI has plunged 20.6%, QBTS has declined 10.4%, and QUBT has fallen 15.9%.

Currently, Wall Street has assigned a "Strong Buy" consensus rating to D-Wave, Rigetti, and IonQ, with RGTI stock offering the highest upside potential among them.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: SoFi Technologies' Earnings Are Coming – Watch These KPIs
data.description:
SoFi Technologies (SOFI) will release its fourth-quarter fiscal 2025 results before the market opens on Friday, January 30. Investors should track SoFi's key performance indicators (KPIs) as they reveal past results and shape future expectations.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

A KPI is a quantifiable metric, such as revenue growth, profit margins, or daily active users, that reveals trends, efficiency, and potential issues like market shifts or resource inefficiencies.

SOFI's Prime KPI: Members

SoFi Technologies is a global fintech platform that offers digital financial services to help users borrow, save, spend, invest, and protect their money. Some of SoFi's KPIs include Members, Loan Origination Type, and Revenue by Segment.

However, SoFi's prime KPI is member growth. It directly fuels product usage and enables revenue scaling through cross-selling and higher lifetime value from engaged users. Tracking total members signals user growth and market penetration.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth
